decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to utilize the powerful Eldrazi creatures and support cards to overwhelm the opponent quickly with aggressive plays. By using cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Glaring Fleshraker to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while also ramping up with Eldrazi Temple and Eye of Ugin, this deck aims to establish a dominant board presence early on. Chalice of the Void and Wasteland are included to disrupt the opponent's spells and mana base, further solidifying the deck's aggressive strategy. The goal is to quickly close out the game before the opponent can effectively respond.

The main focus of this deck is to aggressively control the tempo of the game with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells. By utilizing efficient creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent along with instant speed removal like Lightning Bolt and Cori-Steel Cutter, the deck aims to apply pressure on the opponent while disrupting their game plan. Cards like Daze and Force of Will provide additional disruption and protection, allowing the deck to maintain control of the game and secure a quick victory.

The deck focuses on efficiently disrupting the opponent while developing a strong board presence through aggressive creatures and tempo spells. It aims to control the game by utilizing disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Daze, while applying pressure with cards like Nethergoyf and Murktide Regent. This Dimir Tempo deck aims to quickly close out the game by maintaining an aggressive stance and utilizing powerful threats like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to maintain control of the battlefield.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and lands to control the game by locking out opponents from playing spells effectively. The key strategy involves using cards like Urza's Saga to generate value and maintain board presence, while employing cards like Trinisphere and Pithing Needle to disrupt opponents' game plans. Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms provide additional control options, while cards like Candelabra of Tawnos and Grim Monolith help ramp up mana production. The overall goal is to establish a strong lock on the game through a combination of prison elements and artifact synergies.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by using a mixture of removal spells, counterspells, and card draw to outvalue opponents. It aims to establish control of the board with efficient removal and disruption while generating card advantage. The addition of Yorion as a companion leads to a higher card count, allowing for more flexibility and consistency in maintaining control of the game. The deck includes a variety of lands to support its multicolor strategy, along with powerful threats like Uro to close out the game.

The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly establish board presence with aggressive creatures such as Ocelot Pride and Amped Raptor, backed up by powerful support cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. The deck also utilizes disruption elements like Static Prison and Wasteland to control the board and keep the opponent on the back foot. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast and efficient creatures while disrupting their game plan, ultimately leading to a quick and decisive victory.

The focus of this deck is to control the game through Cradle Control, utilizing cards like Wight of the Reliquary, Gaea's Cradle, and Natural Order to ramp up mana and out-value the opponent. The deck aims to generate a significant advantage by efficiently using mana and creatures to disrupt the opponent's game plan while building towards a powerful finisher like Craterhoof Behemoth. By utilizing powerful creatures and mana acceleration, this deck aims to dominate the board and overwhelm the opponent with its strategic control elements.

This deck focuses on assembling the Dark Depths combo, using cards like Crop Rotation, Thespian's Stage, and Sylvan Safekeeper to create a 20/20 Marit Lage token quickly. The deck also includes utility lands like Maze of Ith and Karakas for protection and control elements like Swords to Plowshares and Gaddock Teeg to disrupt opponents. Elvish Reclaimer helps find key lands while Green Sun's Zenith can tutor for crucial creatures. The deck aims to quickly assemble the combo while controlling the board and disrupting the opponent's strategy.

The deck focuses on quickly deploying threats and putting pressure on the opponent early in the game. It aims to use efficient creatures and mana acceleration to play powerful spells ahead of schedule. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional value and flexibility in the deck. The deck's aggressive strategy is complemented by disruptive elements like Wasteland and Palace Jailer to control the board and maintain tempo. Overall, the deck seeks to overwhelm opponents with its aggressive gameplay and strong synergy between its cards.

This deck focuses on quickly assembling a powerful combo to win the game in a single turn. By using cards like Dark Ritual, Simian Spirit Guide, and Lotus Petal to generate mana, the deck can cast spells like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer to mill the entire library and win with Thassa's Oracle or Dread Return. The deck includes protection in the form of Pact of Negation and disruption with Cabal Therapy. The goal is to execute the combo as quickly and efficiently as possible to catch opponents off guard and secure victory.

The main concept of this deck revolves around locking down the opponent's resources using artifacts in a control-based strategy. By utilizing powerful cards like Urza's Saga, Planar Nexus, and Karn, the Great Creator, the deck aims to create a prison-like environment for the opponent. Cards like Candelabra of Tawnos and Grim Monolith help generate mana for the deck's expensive late-game threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ugin, Eye of the Storms. The inclusion of cards like Trinisphere and Pithing Needle further disrupt the opponent's game plan, making it difficult for them to mount a counterattack.

This UWx Control deck focuses on using counterspells, removal, and card advantage to control the game. It utilizes cards like Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Teferi, Time Raveler to disrupt the opponent's game plan and protect its own. Ponder and Brainstorm provide card selection and deck manipulation, while cards like Wasteland and Stifle help to disrupt the opponent's mana base. The deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent, gradually gaining control of the game until it can secure a victory.

The main focus of this deck is to aggressively reduce your own life total to maximize the power of Death's Shadow as quickly as possible. By utilizing cards like Thoughtseize, Street Wraith, and Fetch Lands, you can lower your life total while disrupting your opponent's game plan. With a combination of efficient removal spells and counterspells, you can protect your threats and push through for powerful swings with Death's Shadow. Additionally, cards like Murktide Regent and Reanimate provide additional threats and ways to close out the game quickly.

This UWx Control deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and powerful planeswalkers. By controlling the board with cards like Swords to Plowshares and Back to Basics, while also drawing cards and filtering through the deck with cards like Brainstorm and Ponder, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent. By utilizing key cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Jace, the Mind Sculptor to generate card advantage and control the tempo of the game, the deck can establish dominance and close out the game.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures such as Griselbrand and Ashen Rider, then bringing them back into play with cards like Reanimate and Animate Dead. By utilizing cards like Faithless Looting and Cabal Therapy to discard key creatures and disrupt opponents, the deck aims to combo off and overwhelm the opponent with an early game advantage. The inclusion of disruptive creatures like Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers and Atraxa, Grand Unifier adds an extra layer of control, allowing for a versatile and aggressive strategy.

This deck focuses on utilizing creatures with disruptive abilities to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with aggressive creatures. The deck is built around the Death & Taxes archetype, which aims to disrupt the opponent's resources and mana base while using efficient creatures to deal damage. The addition of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion adds an extra layer of value by flickering creatures for additional value. The deck aims to generate value through its creatures and disruption spells to outmaneuver the opponent and secure victory.
